title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs sql repositories, pools, transactions, and request consistency from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the context-bound repository and transaction contract. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for context-bound repository and transaction contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Commit Ambiguity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 5466 in a internal administrative API crossing two trust zones. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify transaction outcome without retrying an ambiguous commit; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.

Inspect the Request Commit Ambiguity trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Commit Ambiguity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence. For Go server Separate evidence from confidence · Review Request Commit Ambiguity, keep Request Commit Ambiguity connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
